rag_rules: Repair unresolved references by aligning identifiers with visible AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex scope.
Eliminate clauses containing type <error> unless the root unresolved reference is fixed.
generic_rules: Only reference identifiers that are explicitly declared or visible in the current scope.
Ensure all expressions in contracts have consistent, declared types before using them in comparisons or assignments.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use only int, real, and bool as scalar types in AGREE declarations.
Assignment left-hand sides must refer to existing output features or auxiliary variables declared in the same annex.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the model’s with clauses.
Avoid adding new guarantees or equations to resolve unresolved references—first fix the underlying missing declarations.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
